title: BenchmarkCitation
description: One (benchmark x source document) record in data/citations.jsonl. Topline entity is the BENCHMARK. This file is GENERATED by scripts/build.py from the flat-file inputs (data/registry.yaml, data/documents.yaml, data/extractions/**). Do not hand-edit citations.jsonl; edit the inputs. ALL benchmarks are tracked regardless of Harbor status; on_harbor is just a flag.

benchmark_canonical.description: Normalized via data/registry.yaml aliases (deterministic exact/normalized lookup). null if unmatched (=> needs_review true).

benchmark_raw.description: Verbatim benchmark name as written in the doc (for an unreadable doc, the human-review reason string).

id.description: Document id; joins to data/documents.yaml and data/extractions/<lab>/<id>.yaml.

container.description: Physical form of the document. NOTE: container is NOT the scoring class - a single blog can carry both blog_headliner and model_card mentions (see weight_class).

weight_class.description: Source class of THIS mention, decoupled from the physical doc. blog_headliner = a chart/paragraph about a single benchmark in a release blog; model_card = a multi-benchmark comparison table (standalone card OR a table embedded in a blog); system_card = a row in the long-form system-card paper. null only for unmatched/unreadable placeholder rows.

weight.description: Points for weight_class: blog_headliner 3, model_card 2, system_card 1, else 0. No table-size discount. Aggregators take MAX per (document, model), then sum across documents.

reported.description: Only fields actually stated in the doc. Never infer.

type.description: HOW the benchmark is run: agentic (tools / environment / multi-step) vs chat (one-shot Q&A / exam). From registry.yaml. Kept ORTHOGONAL to domain. null if benchmark unresolved.

domain.description: Subject matter. From registry.yaml. Do NOT conflate with type. null if unresolved.

on_harbor.description: true iff harbor_status == confirmed. Set by sync_harbor.py from the live Harbor registry+adapters.

harbor_status.description: Derived automatically by sync_harbor.py: confirmed iff a live adapter or registry entry matches; otherwise not_in_harbor (a real conversion candidate). Curated name-collision caveats live as `collision:`/`notes:` in registry.yaml, not here.

harbor_name.description: Matched Harbor registry/adapter name, if any (set by sync_harbor.py).

harbor_type.description: native (in registry.json) | adapter (adapters/ dir) | null. Set by sync_harbor.py.

harbor_adapter.description: adapters/<dir> name when harbor_type == adapter; null otherwise. Set by sync_harbor.py.

score_pending.description: true = benchmark matched but numeric score not yet extracted (a to-extract state, NOT a human decision).

needs_review.description: true = genuine HUMAN decision needed (unmatched name / unreadable-or-gated doc). Surfaced as a GitHub issue.

review_issue.description: GitHub issue # (in benchmark-citations) tracking this flag. Issues are the source of truth for the review queue.
